title: Observation
description: The device→brain message: one snapshot of the target device's screen state, sent UP over the Tailscale 8765 channel for the cloud frontier ReAct loop to reason over. Tree-first cadence: ui_tree is always present; screenshot is included only when hasScreenshot is true AND the tree is blind or the model requested it (cuts cloud data + latency).

msg.$comment: MESSAGE-KIND discriminator (I2). Const 'observation' for every device→brain screen-snapshot frame — the SEPARATE key from an action's variant `type`, so message kind and action variant never collide on the wire.
msg.description: Message kind — always 'observation' for a device→brain screen-state frame (vs 'action' / 'action_result').

schema_version.description: Wire-contract version. Also encoded in the /v1/ path of every $id. Additive changes bump the minor; a breaking change bumps the /vN/ path + this major. 1.1 (additive): the intent.name enum grew from 15 to 26 (decision-9 comprehensive intents + open_settings + the guarded send_intent escape-hatch). 1.2 (additive, back-compatible): the new `press_key` action variant (enter/back/home/recents/tab/delete) — enter submits the focused field (ACTION_IME_ENTER), enabling a 'type → submit' flow without a coordinate. 1.3 (M5, additive, back-compatible): the observation `window_topology` array + `posture_changed` flag, and `device_capability.posture` — tablet / large-screen / foldable / DeX display-addressing (multi-window topology + foldable posture re-observe).

ui_tree.description: The actionable accessibility nodes from UiTreeReader.readScreen(), in pre-order DFS, capped at 80. Password fields are redacted at the boundary (see the invariant below).

allOf.$comment: PASSWORD-REDACTION INVARIANT (the whole point of the redaction gate): an is_password node's `text` MUST be the PASSWORD_PLACEHOLDER '·····' (five U+00B7 MIDDLE DOT), never the raw credential. This makes it a hard schema violation for a password node to carry real text on the wire.

screenshot.description: OPTIONAL base64-encoded PNG from the silent AccessibilityService.takeScreenshot(). Present only when device_capability.hasScreenshot is true and the step needs vision (tree-blind surface or model-requested). Password regions are redaction-gated on the device before capture. Ephemeral — never persisted server-side beyond the loop step.

window_topology.description: OPTIONAL (M5.3) — the window layout so the model knows WHICH app owns WHICH on-screen rectangle and where the system bars / split-screen divider sit (node bounds are display-relative). One entry per AccessibilityWindowInfo. On a phone it is typically the app window + status/nav bars; in split-screen / multi-window / DeX it distinguishes the separate app windows and their displayIds.

displayId.description: Display this window is on (AccessibilityWindowInfo.getDisplayId, API 30+). 0 = Display.DEFAULT_DISPLAY; non-zero for a second / external / DeX display.

appPackage.description: Owning app package (from the window root), or "" when unavailable (system/overlay windows or a transient tree). A package name — never screen text.

isSystemBar.description: True for a TYPE_SYSTEM window (status bar / nav bar / system UI) vs an app window.

posture_changed.description: OPTIONAL (M5.5) — true when the foldable POSTURE (device_capability.posture) changed since the previous observation. Prior screen coordinates are INVALID after a posture change; the cloud loop must re-observe before its next coordinate action. ADVISORY today: the M2 loop already re-observes every step, so nothing yet keys behavior off this flag — it exists so a future step-skipping optimization can still force a re-observe across a posture change. Defaults false (no change / non-foldable).

timestamp.description: When this observation was captured. Integer = epoch milliseconds (Android System.currentTimeMillis, the native form); string = RFC 3339 date-time. Used for staleness / re-observe decisions.

allOf.$comment: A device that advertises hasScreenshot=false (e.g. XR) MUST NOT put a screenshot on the wire — the loop is tree-only there.
